diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Chest discomfort is common in panic with hyperventilation and chest-wall tension.
Central chest pain mandates exclusion of a cardiac cause, which requires an ECG and troponin unavailable via telehealth.
Heavy caffeine load is a direct driver of tachycardia and palpitations.
Palpitations with a large stimulant load require ECG to exclude a caffeine-provoked arrhythmia.
Perceived pounding heart is typical of sympathetic panic arousal.
Rapid shallow breathing is a hallmark of acute panic and produces chest-wall tension and paraesthesiae.

symptoms.symptom_name: Fine hand tremor and profuse sweating

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Tremor and diaphoresis are classic features of caffeine intoxication and sympathetic overdrive.
Tremor and tachycardia can reflect thyrotoxicosis; needs examination and thyroid function tests to exclude.

symptoms.symptom_name: Very high recent caffeine intake (four energy drinks in six hours)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: The exposure history is the direct cause of caffeine intoxication and a major contributor to the tachycardia, tremor and chest discomfort.

symptoms.symptom_name: Executive dysfunction — task-initiation failure, mind going blank, inability to sequence steps

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Executive dysfunction / probable ADHD or adjustment disorder
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Chronic difficulty initiating and sequencing administrative tasks, worse under an overwhelming deadline, is the background driver.

symptoms.symptom_name: Reproducible chest-wall tenderness on palpation over the sternum

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Musculoskeletal chest-wall pain from hyperventilation tension
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Reproducible tenderness suggests a musculoskeletal contribution.
Reproducible tenderness lowers but does NOT exclude cardiac pain; this is a sealed in-person finding the AI Doctor cannot obtain and must not assume.
Objective tachycardia, tachypnoea and hypertension confirm autonomic overdrive; sealed clinician measurement, unavailable to the telehealth AI Doctor who has no device reading offered.
Normal oxygenation is reassuring against major respiratory compromise but does not exclude ACS; sealed clinician measurement not available remotely.

investigation_details.expected_result: Likely sinus tachycardia; needed to exclude ischaemia and caffeine-provoked arrhythmia
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: ACS and tachyarrhythmia cannot be excluded — the reason in-person assessment is required.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: An ECG is the first-line test to exclude the cardiac mimic and cannot be obtained via telehealth.

investigation_details.expected_result: Expected normal, but required to exclude myocardial injury
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Myocardial injury cannot be excluded remotely.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Troponin is required to exclude myocardial injury and is a hospital investigation.

symptom_clusters.target_diagnosis: Acute panic/anxiety reaction compounded by caffeine intoxication
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: The chest pain, palpitations, hyperventilation and tremor cluster fits sympathetic overdrive — but the same cluster is exactly what makes a cardiac mimic dangerous to dismiss remotely.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: First-line to exclude ACS and caffeine-provoked arrhythmia given chest pain with resting tachycardia — requires in-person assessment.
To exclude myocardial injury; part of the urgent in-person cardiac workup.
To exclude thyrotoxicosis as a cause of tachycardia and tremor, once the acute cardiac cause is excluded.
